lines: # Add scripts so that we can use them inside the container
description: "Whether to run Go SDK e2e tests (true/false)"
description: "Whether to run OpenLineage e2e tests (true/false)"

-        description: "JSON matrix of released Airflow versions for provider e2e compat tests."
+        description: "JSON matrix of released Airflow versions for the OpenLineage e2e compat tests."
+    name: "OpenLineage e2e tests with PROD image"
+  # Older-Airflow compatibility: costly, so it does not run on every OpenLineage PR — only on canary
+  # (scheduled / main) or when a maintainer sets the "full tests needed" label (both folded into the
+  # run-openlineage-e2e-compat-tests selective-check output). PRs otherwise run the PROD job only.
+    name: "OpenLineage e2e compat tests with PROD image"
name: "Chromium UI e2e tests with PROD image"

-        description: "Display name for the provider, used only in the job name."
-        description: "The array of labels (in json form) determining runners."
-        description: "Which version of python should be used by default"

description: "The array of labels (in json form) determining runners."

+        description: "Released Airflow version to test with current providers from main."
-      # Gating on job-level `if:` instead of per-step would skip the whole job — and GitHub Actions
-      # does not evaluate a dynamic `name:` (the one above, using `inputs.*`) for a skipped job, so it
-      # renders the raw unevaluated template instead. Gating each step keeps the job itself "running"
-      # (and its name properly resolved), ending in success with every real step skipped for AF2.
+      # OpenLineage requires Airflow 3.x. Gating each step (rather than the whole job) keeps the job
+      # itself "running" so its dynamic name resolves, ending in success with every real step skipped
-        run: echo "Skipping $PROVIDER e2e tests — not supported against Airflow 2.x."
+        run: echo "Skipping OpenLineage e2e tests — not supported against Airflow 2.x."
+      - name: "Run OpenLineage e2e compat tests against Airflow ${{ inputs.airflow-version }}"

+# Docker Compose override for the openlineage E2E test mode.
+# The OpenLineage system-test DAGs are sourced into the dags folder by
+# test VariableTransport (events land in Airflow Variables) and each DAG's terminal
+# OpenLineageTestOperator validates them, so a run ending "success" means the lineage matched.
+  # These DAGs are the only ones the harness drives; keep the stock example DAGs out of the way.
+  # The OpenLineage system-test DAGs (and the custom VariableTransport / OpenLineageTestOperator
+  # they import) live under <dags>/system/openlineage; the dags folder is added to PYTHONPATH so
+  # both DAG parsing and the dotted-path transport class resolve in every component.
+  # Route OpenLineage events to the test VariableTransport (stores events in Airflow Variables,
+  # read back and validated by the in-DAG OpenLineageTestOperator). The transport type is the
+  # fully-qualified class path; the OpenLineage client imports it directly.
+  # Deployment-wide policy required by the two conf-driven policy DAGs. Each rule is scoped by
+  # dag_id, so both rules coexist and do not affect any other DAG.
+  # Both control how quickly the dag-processor picks up dags: REFRESH_INTERVAL is how often it
+  # re-scans the folder for new/removed files, MIN_FILE_PROCESS_INTERVAL is how often it re-parses
+  # a file it already knows about. Tightened from the 300s/30s defaults so the e2e run doesn't wait
+  # on the dag-processor's own cadence on top of everything else.
+  # openlineage_defer_simple_dag's TimeDeltaSensor defaults to a 180s delta so it reliably defers at
+  # least once even before a worker picks up the task; tighten it here since we do not need to wait.
+  # minutes; tighten it here since we do not need to wait for worker.
+  # Serve the dags folder through a versioned bundle so example_openlineage_versioned_dag gets a
+  # non-null dag_bundle_version. MockVersionedLocalDagBundle still serves from /opt/airflow/dags
+  # (keeping PYTHONPATH valid); it is copied into the dags folder by prepare_dags.py.
+  # The breeze PROD image installs every provider from source, including informatica, whose listener
+  # fires on every task and errors without its connection — suppressing OL events. A curated
+  # deployment (e.g. Astro) does not ship it; disable its listener to match.
+    # example_openlineage_docs_file_dag sets doc_md="dag_doc.md", which Airflow resolves with a
+    # CWD-relative open(); components run with CWD=/opt/airflow. prepare_dags.py generates a clean,
+    # license-free dag_doc.md (matching the expected event) in the dags folder; expose it here.
+    """Set up the openlineage E2E test mode.
+    The OpenLineage system-test DAGs are the single source of truth; ``prepare_dags`` copies them
+    into the stack's dags folder (stripping the pytest-only footer) alongside the harness-only warmup
+    DAG and versioned bundle. The ``openlineage.yml`` overlay carries the OpenLineage env config and
+    mounts the generated ``dag_doc.md`` where the docs DAG resolves it.
+    console.print("[yellow]Preparing OpenLineage DAGs from the provider system tests...")
+    # openlineage sources its dags from the provider system tests (via _setup_openlineage_integration),
+    # so it must not also load the stock e2e dags — the harness triggers every dag it finds.
# Please Do not use this Fernet key in any deployments! Please generate your own key.
+    """Live host path of the stack's task logs (bind-mounted), readable while tests run."""
+    """Host path of the dags served to the stack."""
+# OpenLineage E2E test paths. The DAGs are sourced from the provider system tests at runtime by
+# openlineage_tests/prepare_dags.py; the overlay carries the OpenLineage-specific env + dag_doc mount.
+        # DELETE and other no-content responses (204) have an empty body; json() would choke on it.
+        return response.json() if response.content else None
@@ -24,23 +24,30 @@ provider's system-test DAGs against it — exercising the integration the way it
deployment (separate scheduler / dag-processor / triggerer / api-server / workers), rather than the
-See the [top-level README](../README.md) for how to run any provider's e2e suite with breeze, how
-this is wired into CI, and what's involved in adding a new provider.
+They run as the `openlineage` mode of the shared `airflow-e2e-tests` suite, reusing its
- The DAGs, `OpenLineageTestOperator`, `VariableTransport`, and expected-event JSON are the ones in
that directory stays the single source of truth. `prepare_dags.py` copies them into a (gitignored)
`dags/` folder at runtime and strips the pytest-only `get_test_run` footer so they parse in a
+  overlay (OpenLineage env config + `dag_doc.md` mount) on top of the shared compose base; the
- OpenLineage is configured to emit through the test `VariableTransport` (events land in Airflow
Variables); each DAG's terminal `OpenLineageTestOperator` task validates the emitted events against
the expected templates. **A DAG run that ends `success` means its lineage matched.**
- `harness.py` triggers every DAG through the REST API, waits for completion, retries a failed DAG
once, and asserts all expected DAGs succeeded.
-- A small `MockVersionedLocalDagBundle` provides a versioned bundle (so `dag_bundle_version` is
-  populated) while still serving DAGs from the local dags folder.
+- A small `MockVersionedLocalDagBundle` (in `dags_extra/`) provides a versioned bundle (so
+  `dag_bundle_version` is populated) while still serving DAGs from the local dags folder.
@@ -48,16 +55,30 @@ this is wired into CI, and what's involved in adding a new provider.
offenders, e.g. `... not successful: {'openlineage_docs_file_dag': 'failed'}`, and logs
`⚠ DAGs that passed only on retry (flaky first run): [...]`.
- **On failure, the relevant task logs are printed right in the pytest output** (`check_events` plus
-  any task that didn't succeed, filtered to warning/error lines — see `tests/task_logs.py`) — no
-  need to open the log artifact for the common case, e.g. the exact mismatch
+  any task that didn't succeed, filtered to warning/error lines — see `task_logs.py`) — no need to
+  open the log artifact for the common case, e.g. the exact mismatch
(``Path `job > facets > ...`: expected X but got Y``).
-- **Per-DAG logs** are still written in full to `logs/dag_id=<dag>/.../task_id=<task>/attempt=1.log`
-  for deeper digging beyond what gets printed.
+- **Per-DAG logs** are still written in full under `airflow-e2e-tests/logs/` for deeper digging
- With `--skip-docker-compose-deletion`, the stack stays up: get a token from
`curl -s http://localhost:8080/auth/token` and check `GET /api/v2/importErrors` for DAG parse
errors, or browse the runs in the UI at <http://localhost:8080> (login `airflow` / `airflow`).
+A compat matrix reruns the suite against older *released* Airflow versions with the current provider
+code from main — the same idea as the provider compatibility tests, catching core-vs-provider
+breakages early. It builds a lightweight image (`apache/airflow:<version>` + current
+The matrix is costly, so it does **not** run on every OpenLineage PR: it runs on canary (scheduled /
+main) or when a maintainer sets the **`full tests needed`** label on the PR (both folded into the
+`run-openlineage-e2e-compat-tests` selective-check output). Ordinary PRs run the PROD-image job only.
Some DAGs require a newer Airflow than the compat targets and are dropped for older versions in
@@ -66,10 +87,13 @@ Some DAGs require a newer Airflow than the compat targets and are dropped for ol
-docker-compose.yaml         # the deployed stack (postgres + apiserver + scheduler + dag-processor + triggerer)
-docker-compose-local.yaml   # overlay that mounts local sources (used unless --skip-mounting-local-volumes)
-Dockerfile                  # lightweight image for --airflow-version: apache/airflow:<ver> + current providers
prepare_dags.py             # sources DAGs from the provider system tests into dags/ at runtime
+harness.py                  # triggers every DAG via the REST API and collects final run states
+task_logs.py                # locates and prints the relevant task logs on failure
dags_extra/                 # harness-only DAGs/modules (warmup DAG, versioned bundle) copied into dags/
-tests/                      # conftest (compose lifecycle), harness, constants, and the pytest entrypoint
